summary: Listener is in the final ~4 weeks before Maryland's June 23, 2026 primary and the through-line is differentiation: he wants the 17 At-Large Council Democrats compared head-to-head, especially on sanctuary policy, MCPS funding/Wootton, and property taxes. He has repeatedly asked for full lifelong records tied to named candidates, not recent-vote snapshots. NOTE: daily_notes are stale — the newest row is 2026-04-30 — so these themes carry forward prior asks rather than fresh input. Coverage is now ahead of schedule (every candidate through 06-10 has episodes on disk), so the priority shifts to dossiers+episodes for the first uncovered At-Large names (Silvestre, Khan, Goldberg).
themes: Side-by-side differentiation of the 17 At-Large County Council Democrats | listener's #1 recurring ask: a comparison matrix, not 17 standalone bios
Which candidates actually oppose vs. defend the sanctuary / ICE-cooperation framework | listener asked directly (04-24) and it is still unanswered for the At-Large field
Full lifelong voting record & viewpoint per candidate, not just recent votes | listener complained episodes never tied specific old news to a specific candidate (04-25/04-26)
MCPS funding as line-item votes + the new school-rating methodology and per-school Rockville grades | the lever behind the Wootton High closure/relocation question (04-29/04-30)
Property-tax burden and non-essential-spending discipline | listener thinks property tax is too high and supports cutting non-essential spend (04-27/04-28)
open_questions: Who among my candidates is against sanctuary policy on illegal immigration?
Why do they want to close and move Wootton High School?
Is Andrew Friedson actually the fiscal-restraint candidate, measured by line-item votes?
What are the policy differences between each candidate?
Deep dive of the new Montgomery County Public School rating system and per-school grades for Rockville schools
underserved_topics: The At-Large field beyond the front-runners: Karla Silvestre, Hamza Khan, Scott Goldberg, Fatmata Barrie and the rest have no dossier or episode yet
Ben Kramer's full voting record on education-funding increases (listener dislikes his yes-to-every-increase pattern, 04-27)
Per-school MCPS rating/grade data for the specific Rockville schools the listener's household attends
Non-partisan and down-ballot races the listener still must decide: judicial, Register of Wills, Clerk of Circuit Court, Republican alternatives
avoid_list: 'Thirty-X days out from the Maryland Democratic primary' cold-open device — used 2026-05-16/17/18/19 ep1 and again 2026-05-25 Peter James ep1
'Montgomery County native' framing hook — used heavily 2026-05-16 Friedson ep1
'My Seven Black Fathers' / Reid Temple A.M.E. memoir-anchor for anyone other than Jawando — used 2026-05-19 ep1
'Anniston, Alabama / Donoho School / Vaughn Stewart Jr as mayor' formative device for anyone other than Vaughn Stewart — used 2026-05-20 ep1
'Born in Trenton / adopted as an infant from a teenage mom in a convent' origin device for anyone other than Dawn Luedtke — used 2026-05-17 ep1
'First Muslim officer of the Montgomery County Police Department' identity device for anyone other than Sharif Hidayat — used 2026-05-18 ep1
'Pre-political biography is thinner in the public record / no Wikipedia entry' candidate qualifier — used 2026-05-16 ep1
ICE Out Act / Trust Act / DHS officer screening bill as the immigration framing hook — used 2026-05-21 Glass ep3/ep4
'Run the buses, teach the kids, pick up the trash' tagline — used 2026-05-21 Glass ep1/ep2/ep4
'Welcome back ... It is Monday May twenty-fifth ... one month from tomorrow' calendar/countdown studio open — used 2026-05-25 Peter James ep1
Generic 'Welcome back to the Rockville Primary Briefing, I'm Alex' studio open — used 2026-05-27 Sayles ep1
'Brass plaque on Maryland Avenue in Rockville listing the County Executives' cold-open — used 2026-05-29 Marc Elrich ep1
'Day two / day three / day four of [Candidate] week' episode-open device — used heavily 2026-05-29 Marc Elrich set

Marc-Elrich-specific framings ('teacher and tenant organizer from Takoma Park', 'won by 32 votes', 'lighter than the council, louder than the council') — reserve for Elrich only, do not reuse for other candidates
RE-COVERING MARC ELRICH AT ALL — fully covered 2026-05-29 (4 valid episodes on disk). Gap-bug guard: do not let an unscheduled-gap day re-queue Elrich (it did on both 05-29 and 05-30).
